title: 会社の役職 — Company Positions
meaning: Common company job titles and positions
explanation: Knowing the hierarchy of company positions is essential for using correct keigo and understanding who to address in different situations.
examples: [{"japanese":"社長","reading":"しゃちょう","meaning":"President/ CEO"},{"japanese":"取締役","reading":"とりしまりやく","meaning":"Board member/ Director"},{"japanese":"部長","reading":"ぶちょう","meaning":"Department Manager"},{"japanese":"課長","reading":"かちょう","meaning":"Section Manager"},{"japanese":"係長","reading":"かかりちょう","meaning":"Subsection Chief"},{"japanese":"主任","reading":"しゅにん","meaning":"Team Leader/ Supervisor"},{"japanese":"先輩","reading":"せんぱい","meaning":"Senior colleague"},{"japanese":"後輩","reading":"こうはい","meaning":"Junior colleague"},{"japanese":"同僚","reading":"どうりょう","meaning":"Coworker"},{"japanese":"上司","reading":"じょうし","meaning":"Superior/ Boss"}]

title: 会社の部署 — Company Departments
meaning: Common department names in a company
explanation: Knowing department names helps you navigate company structure, understand meeting contexts, and address correspondence correctly.
examples: [{"japanese":"営業部","reading":"えいぎょうぶ","meaning":"Sales Department"},{"japanese":"経理部","reading":"けいりぶ","meaning":"Accounting Department"},{"japanese":"人事部","reading":"じんじぶ","meaning":"Human Resources Department"},{"japanese":"総務部","reading":"そうむぶ","meaning":"General Affairs Department"},{"japanese":"開発部","reading":"かいはつぶ","meaning":"Development Department"},{"japanese":"マーケティング部","reading":"マーケティングぶ","meaning":"Marketing Department"},{"japanese":"企画部","reading":"きかくぶ","meaning":"Planning Department"},{"japanese":"購買部","reading":"こうばいぶ","meaning":"Purchasing Department"},{"japanese":"法務部","reading":"ほうむぶ","meaning":"Legal Department"}]

title: 会議の日本語 — Meeting Japanese
reading: かいぎでつかうひょうげん
explanation: Meetings in Japan follow specific protocols. Knowing these phrases helps you participate effectively and show professionalism.
examples: [{"japanese":"只今から会議を始めます。","reading":"ただいまからかいぎをはじめます。","meaning":"We will begin the meeting now."},{"japanese":"議題に入る前に、","reading":"ぎだいにはいるまえに、","meaning":"Before we get into the agenda,"},{"japanese":"ご意見をお聞かせください。","reading":"ごいけんをおきかせください。","meaning":"Please share your opinions."},{"japanese":"私の考えを申し上げます。","reading":"わたしのかんがえをもうしあげます。","meaning":"Let me share my thoughts."},{"japanese":"賛成です。／反対です。","reading":"さんせいです。／はんたいです。","meaning":"I agree. / I disagree."},{"japanese":"一旦持ち帰って検討します。","reading":"いったんもちかえってけんとうします。","meaning":"Let me take this back and consider it."},{"japanese":"本日はお忙しい中お集まりいただき、ありがとうございます。","reading":"ほんじつはおいそがしいなかおあつまりいただき、ありがとうございます。","meaning":"Thank you all for taking time from your busy schedules to gather here today."}]

title: ビジネスメールの表現 — Business Email Phrases
japanese: メールでよく使うフレーズ
reading: メールでよくつかうフレーズ
explanation: Japanese business emails follow a strict format: opening greeting, introduction, main content, closing remark, and signature. The level of formality depends on the relationship.
examples: [{"japanese":"件名：○○の件について","reading":"けんめい：○○のけんについて","meaning":"Subject: Regarding ○○"},{"japanese":"平素より大変お世話になっております。","reading":"へいそよりたいへんおせわになっております。","meaning":"Thank you for your continued support (formal)."},{"japanese":"添付ファイルをご確認ください。","reading":"てんぷファイルをごかくにんください。","meaning":"Please check the attached file."},{"japanese":"ご不明な点がございましたら、お気軽にご連絡ください。","reading":"ごふめいなてんがございましたら、おきがるにごれんらくください。","meaning":"If you have any questions, please feel free to contact us."},{"japanese":"今後ともよろしくお願いいたします。","reading":"こんごともよろしくおねがいいたします。","meaning":"We look forward to your continued support."},{"japanese":"取り急ぎご連絡まで。","reading":"とりいそぎごれんらくまで。","meaning":"Just a quick note (closing for brief/ urgent emails)."}]

title: 取引先とのやり取り — Client Interactions
reading: おきゃくさまとのかいわ
explanation: When interacting with clients (取引先), always use the highest level of keigo. Refer to your own company humbly and the client's company with respect.
examples: [{"japanese":"いつも大変お世話になっております。","reading":"いつもたいへんおせわになっております。","meaning":"Thank you very much for your continued support."},{"japanese":"弊社の製品についてご紹介させていただきます。","reading":"へいしゃのせいひんについてごしょうかいさせていただきます。","meaning":"Allow me to introduce our company's products."},{"japanese":"ご要望に沿えるよう努めます。","reading":"ごようぼうにそえるようつとめます。","meaning":"We will do our best to meet your requests."},{"japanese":"大変申し訳ございませんでした。","reading":"たいへんもうしわけございませんでした。","meaning":"We deeply apologize (for a mistake/ delay)."},{"japanese":"ご検討のほど、よろしくお願いいたします。","reading":"ごけんとうのほど、よろしくおねがいいたします。","meaning":"We would appreciate your consideration."}]

title: ビジネス敬語フレーズ集 — Essential Keigo Phrases
japanese: 絶対覚えたい敬語フレーズ
reading: ぜったいおぼえたいけいごフレーズ
meaning: Essential keigo phrases every business person must know
explanation: These are the most commonly used keigo phrases in daily business interactions. Master these to sound professional and respectful in any Japanese workplace.
examples: [{"japanese":"お世話になっております","reading":"おせわになっております","meaning":"Thank you for your support (standard greeting for business partners)"},{"japanese":"かしこまりました","reading":"かしこまりました","meaning":"Certainly / Understood (polite acknowledgment)"},{"japanese":"承知しました","reading":"しょうちしました","meaning":"Understood / Acknowledged (formal)"},{"japanese":"恐れ入ります","reading":"おそれいります","meaning":"Excuse me / I'm sorry (polite, used to get attention or apologize)"},{"japanese":"お手数をお掛けします","reading":"おてすうをおかけします","meaning":"Sorry for the trouble (when asking someone to do something)"},{"japanese":"ご確認ください","reading":"ごかくにんください","meaning":"Please confirm / Please check"},{"japanese":"よろしくお願いいたします","reading":"よろしくおねがいいたします","meaning":"I look forward to working with you / Please handle this (standard closing)"},{"japanese":"失礼いたします","reading":"しつれいいたします","meaning":"Excuse me / Goodbye (polite, when leaving)"}]
